channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Just 32 percent in US trust AI: Study
item.description: Only about a third of Americans trust artificial intelligence (AI), according to new data from the Edelman Trust Barometer. Just 32 percent of people in the U.S. said they trust the rapidly developing technology, according to the annual global survey’s “Insights for the Technology Sector.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Local history buffs are turning to Facebook to share memories of bygone eras
item.description: Social media has a reputation for capturing ephemeral thoughts and images, but around the world, people are using Facebook for a different purpose, setting up groups to record and share images and memories of the past.
item.dc:creator: Steven Melendez, Fast Company

item.title: TikTok returns to Apple and Google app stores in the US
item.description: HONG KONG (AP) — TikTok has returned to the app stores of Apple and Google in the U.S., after President Donald Trump delayed the enforcement of a TikTok ban. TikTok, which is operated by Chinese technology firm ByteDance, was removed from Apple and Google’s app stores on Jan.
item.dc:creator: ZEN SOO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Palo Alto Networks firewall bug being exploited by threat actors: Report
item.description: Admins with firewalls from Palo Alto Networks should make sure the devices are fully patched and the management interface blocked from open internet access after the discovery this week of a zero-day login authentication bypass in the PAN-OS operating system.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Apple Watch speaker not working? Here's what you should do – according to Apple
item.description: Apple has published a support document following a raft of issues reported with Apple Watch speakers.
item.dc:creator: stephen.warwick@futurenet.com (Stephen Warwick), TechRadar

item.title: Zacks Investment hit in data breach - 12 million users potentially at risk
item.description: The company hasn't commented on the allegations yet, but users may wish to up their security protection.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Arm secures Meta as first customer in chip push, challenging industry giants
item.description: In a landmark shift, Arm has secured Meta as the first major customer for its internally designed server CPUs, a move that signals its entry into direct chip sales and places it in direct competition with its biggest customers, including Qualcomm and Nvidia.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Captain America: Brave New World ending explained: does Sam Wilson beat Red Hulk, is there a post-credits scene, are there any Marvel cameos, and more big questions answered
item.description: Captain America 4's ending sets up some interesting MCU story threads – here's what it means for the next batch of Marvel movies.
item.dc:creator: tom.power@futurenet.com (Tom Power), TechRadar

item.title: Your Kindle Scribe just became a better note-taking ereader with promised AI features rolling out widely now
item.description: The latest firmware brings AI Summary and handwriting recognition to old and new Kindle Scribes in three more countries.
item.dc:creator: sharmishta.sarkar@futurenet.com (Sharmishta Sarkar), TechRadar

item.title: Apple has been charged with abuse of power by this German watchdog. Here’s why
item.description: The German antitrust authority has charged Apple with abusing its market power through its app tracking tool and giving itself preferential treatment in a move that could result in daily fines for the iPhone maker if it fails to change its business practices.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: JetBrains’ Ktor adds CLI for simpler project creation
item.description: JetBrains’ Ktor 3.1.0, an update to the Kotlin-based framework for asynchronous server-side and client-side applications, is now available, featuring a command-line tool for simpler creation of projects.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Scout Motors' effort to directly sell its electric SUVs where they'll make them stalls
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — Legislation to let Volkswagen-backed Scout Motors sell its electric SUVs directly to people in South Carolina, where the vehicles will be built, has stalled after fierce opposition from traditional automobile dealers.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: EU pulls back – for the moment – on privacy and genAI liability compliance regulations
item.description: When the EU on Tuesday said it was not, at this time, moving ahead with critical legislation involving privacy and genAI liability issues, it honestly reported that members couldn’t agree. But the reasons why they couldn’t agree get much more complicated.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Thomson Reuters scores early win in AI copyright battles in the US
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Thomson Reuters has won an early battle in court over the question of fair use in artificial intelligence-related copyright cases.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: BBC: Chatbots distort the facts about news
item.description: It’s already known that today’s generative AI (genAI) tools often have trouble with basic facts. Now, it’s clear they don’t well with current events either.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Apple’s Chinese AI problem (perhaps) solved with Alibaba
item.description: Reflecting the erosion of universality, Apple Intelligence will now be coming to China, but rather than working with a US AI partner, the company will use Chinese-made AI tech from Alibaba.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: 3 reasons Microsoft needn’t fear DeepSeek
item.description: The release of the latest version of the Chinese genAI bot DeepSeek last month upended the tech world when its creators claimed it was built for only $6 million — far less than the hundreds of billions of dollars Microsoft, Google, OpenAI, Meta, and others have poured into genAI development.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Esports Olympics to debut in Saudi Arabia later than expected in 2027
item.description: LAUSANNE, Switzerland (AP) — The first Olympic Esports Games will be hosted in Riyadh in 2027, two years later than expected when a 12-year deal with Saudi Arabia was signed last year.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Trump's AI ambition and China's DeepSeek overshadow an AI summit in Paris
item.description: PARIS (AP) — The geopolitics of artificial intelligence will be in focus at a major summit in France where world leaders, executives and experts will hammer out pledges on guiding the development of the rapidly advancing technology.
item.dc:creator: SYLVIE CORBET and KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Top 20 apps tracking you every day
item.description: The apps collecting the most data about you are among the most widely used. Kurt the CyberGuy takes a look at 20 of these apps and how you can protect personal information.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Huge healthcare data breach exposes over 1 million Americans' sensitive information
item.description: Community Health Center, a Connecticut-based healthcare provider, has disclosed a recent data breach affecting over 1 million people in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
